---
name: avm-child-module-publishing
description: "Publish Bicep child modules to the Azure Verified Modules (AVM) public registry. Covers the full end-to-end workflow: prerequisite verification, allowed-list registration, telemetry instrumentation, version file creation, changelog updates, parent module updates, and final validation. USE FOR: publish child module, add child module telemetry, child module version.json, child module CHANGELOG, child module allowed list, bicep child module publishing. DO NOT USE FOR: creating new child module functionality (use AVM-Plan/AVM-Implement agents), publishing top-level parent modules, Terraform modules."
---

# AVM Bicep Child Module Publishing

> **AUTHORITATIVE GUIDANCE — MANDATORY COMPLIANCE**
>
> This skill implements the official [Child Module Publishing](https://azure.github.io/Azure-Verified-Modules/contributing/bicep/bicep-contribution-flow/child-module-publishing/) workflow from Azure Verified Modules. Follow these steps exactly. Do not skip or reorder steps.

---

## Triggers

Activate this skill when user wants to:

- Publish an existing Bicep child module to the AVM public registry
- Enable a child module for independent publishing
- Add telemetry to a child module
- Register a child module in the allowed list

---

## Context

Child resources exist only within the scope of another resource (e.g., a subnet within a virtual network). In AVM, child modules deploy these child resources and live inside their parent module's folder structure. By default, child modules are **not** independently published — they must be explicitly published.

Publishing a child module does **not** move or reorganize any files. The child module stays in its existing folder within the parent.

> **Important**: This process is currently in pilot/preview phase. Only resource modules (`avm/res/`) are supported. Pattern and utility modules are not supported.

---

## Rules

1. **Verify all prerequisites before implementation** — Do not start code changes until all 3 prerequisites are confirmed.
2. **Never move or reorganize module folders** — Publishing does not change folder hierarchy.
3. **Use exact templates** — Telemetry blocks, version.json, and changelog formats must match the canonical templates exactly.
4. **Maintain alphabetical order** — When editing the allowed list JSON.
5. **Run Set-AVMModule** — Always run the utility on ALL modules that have a main.bicep file updated by this workflow before committing.
6. **Never edit README.md directly** — READMEs are regenerated by Set-AVMModule.
7. **Existing tests do not need changes** — Publishing changes (telemetry, version file, changelogs) don't affect test cases.

---

## Workflow

### PHASE 1: Prerequisites Verification

Before any implementation, verify **all three** prerequisites. If any are missing, stop and instruct the user on how to resolve.

#### Step 1.1 — Verify Bicep Child Module Proposal Issue

Check that an [AVM issue](https://github.com/Azure/Azure-Verified-Modules/issues?q=is%3Aissue+state%3Aopen+label%3A%22Class%3A+Child+Module+%3Apackage%3A%22+label%3A%22Language%3A+Bicep+%3Amuscle%3A%22) exists proposing this child module for publishing.

- **If missing**: Direct the user to create one using the [Bicep Child Module Proposal template](https://github.com/Azure/Azure-Verified-Modules/issues/new?template=4_module_proposal_bicep_child.yml).
- **If just created**: Allow a few days for the core team to process before continuing.

#### Step 1.2 — Verify Telemetry ID Prefix

Look up the child module in the Bicep Resource Module Index CSV:

- **URL**: `https://github.com/Azure/Azure-Verified-Modules/blob/main/docs/static/module-indexes/BicepResourceModules.csv`
- **Local path**: `Azure-Verified-Modules/docs/static/module-indexes/BicepResourceModules.csv`

Search for the child module name in the `ModuleName` field and note the `TelemetryIdPrefix` value.

**Format**: The telemetry ID prefix follows the pattern `<4-char-hex>bcp.res.<provider>-<resourcetype>` (e.g., `46d3xbcp.res.network-virtualnetworksubnet`).

- **If not found**: Instruct the user to reach out to `@Azure/avm-core-team-technical-bicep` via the proposal issue.

#### Step 1.3 — Verify MAR-file Registration

Confirm the child module is registered in the [MAR-file](https://github.com/microsoft/mcr/blob/main/teams/bicep/bicep.yml).

- **Local path** (if available): `mcr/teams/bicep/bicep.yml`
- Search for `public/bicep/<child-module-path>` (e.g., `public/bicep/avm/res/network/virtual-network/subnet`).

The MAR entry follows this format:

```yaml
- name: public/bicep/<child-module-path>
  displayName: <Parent DisplayName> - <Child DisplayName>
  description: AVM Child Module for <Parent DisplayName> - <Child DisplayName>
  logoUrl: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/bicep/main/src/vscode-bicep/icons/bicep-logo-256.png
  supportLink: https://github.com/Azure/bicep-registry-modules/issues
  documentationLink: https://github.com/Azure/bicep-registry-modules/tree/main/<child-module-path>/README.md
```

- **If not found**: The MAR-file can only be modified by Microsoft FTEs. Instruct the user to reach out to `@Azure/avm-core-team-technical-bicep` or the parent module owner.

---

### PHASE 2: Implementation

Once all prerequisites are verified, implement the following changes in order.

#### Step 2.1 — Add to Allowed List

**File**: `utilities/pipelines/staticValidation/compliance/helper/child-module-publish-allowed-list.json`

Add the child module path to the `allowed-child-modules` array, maintaining **alphabetical order**.

```json
{
  "allowed-child-modules": [
    ...existing entries...,
    "<child-module-path>"
  ]
}
```

Example path: `avm/res/network/virtual-network/subnet`

#### Step 2.2 — Add Telemetry to Child Module main.bicep

**File**: `<child-module-path>/main.bicep`

##### 2.2a — Add enableTelemetry parameter

Add this parameter to the child module's `main.bicep`. Place it after other parameters, before variables:

```bicep
@description('Optional. Enable/Disable usage telemetry for module.')
param enableTelemetry bool = true
```

##### 2.2b — Add avmTelemetry deployment resource

Add the telemetry resource block. Replace `<TelemetryIdPrefix>` with the value from the CSV (Step 1.2):

```bicep
#disable-next-line no-deployments-resources
resource avmTelemetry 'Microsoft.Resources/deployments@2024-03-01' = if (enableTelemetry) {
  name: '<TelemetryIdPrefix>.${replace('-..--..-', '.', '-')}.${substring(uniqueString(deployment().name), 0, 4)}'
  properties: {
    mode: 'Incremental'
    template: {
      '$schema': 'https://schema.management.azure.com/schemas/2019-04-01/deploymentTemplate.json#'
      contentVersion: '1.0.0.0'
      resources: []
      outputs: {
        telemetry: {
          type: 'String'
          value: 'For more information, see https://aka.ms/avm/TelemetryInfo'
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

> **Placement**: Insert right after the variables section, before the first existing resource declaration. Use the `#disable-next-line no-deployments-resources` comment on the line immediately before the resource.

#### Step 2.3 — Update Parent Module main.bicep

**File**: `<parent-module-path>/main.bicep` (the direct parent of the child module)

##### 2.3a — Add enableReferencedModulesTelemetry variable

If not already present, add this variable:

```bicep
var enableReferencedModulesTelemetry = false
```

> **Placement**: Place it near other variable declarations, typically before `builtInRoleNames`.

##### 2.3b — Pass telemetry flag to child module deployment

Find the module deployment that references the child module and add:

```bicep
enableTelemetry: enableReferencedModulesTelemetry
```

> **Important**: If the parent already has `enableReferencedModulesTelemetry` (because other child modules are already published), skip 2.3a but still ensure 2.3b is added for the new child module deployment.

> **Nested parents**: If there are intermediate parent modules between the top-level parent and the child, each intermediate parent must also have the `enableReferencedModulesTelemetry` variable and pass it down.

#### Step 2.4 — Add version.json

**File**: `<child-module-path>/version.json`

Create this file with exactly this content:

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://aka.ms/bicep-registry-module-version-file-schema#",
  "version": "0.1"
}
```

#### Step 2.5 — Add CHANGELOG.md to Child Module

**File**: `<child-module-path>/CHANGELOG.md`

Create with this content, replacing `<child-module-path>` with the actual path:

```markdown
# Changelog

The latest version of the changelog can be found [here](https://github.com/Azure/bicep-registry-modules/blob/main/<child-module-path>/CHANGELOG.md).

## 0.1.0

### Changes

- Initial version

### Breaking Changes

- None
```

#### Step 2.6 — Update Parent Changelogs

Update the CHANGELOG.md of **every versioned parent** up to the top-level parent, adding a new **patch** version entry:

```markdown
## <CurrentMajor>.<CurrentMinor>.<CurrentPatch+1>

### Changes

- Enabling child module `<child-module-path>` for publishing (added telemetry option)

### Breaking Changes

- None
```

> **Read the existing changelog first** to determine the current latest version and increment the patch number.

---

### PHASE 3: Validation & PR

#### Step 3.1 — Run Set-AVMModule

Always run the Set-AVMModule utility pointing to ALL modules (parent and child) that have a main.bicep file updated by this workflow:

```powershell
Set-AVMModule -InvokeForDiff
```

> Use `-SkipFileAndFolderSetup -ThrottleLimit 5` parameters when running locally to update an existing module.

This regenerates READMEs and compiles Bicep files for the entire module tree.

#### Step 3.2 — Test Locally

ALWAYS run static validation tests locally before completing this workflow:

```powershell
./utilities/tools/Test-ModuleLocally.ps1 -TemplateFilePath '<path-to-top-level-parent>/main.bicep' -PesterTest
```

> Existing tests do not need updating — the publishing changes don't affect test cases.

---

## Decision Points

| Situation                                             | Action                                                          |
| ----------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Proposal issue missing                                | Stop — user must create it first                                |
| Telemetry ID prefix missing                           | Stop — user must contact core team                              |
| MAR-file entry missing                                | Stop — user must contact core team (requires MS FTE)            |
| Parent already has `enableReferencedModulesTelemetry` | Skip adding the variable, just wire it to the new child         |
| Multiple levels of nesting                            | Each intermediate parent needs the variable and pass-through    |
| Child module already has `enableTelemetry` param      | Verify the telemetry resource block also exists; add if missing |

---

## Files Modified (Summary)

| #   | File                                                             | Change                                                    |
| --- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1   | `utilities/pipelines/.../child-module-publish-allowed-list.json` | Add child module path                                     |
| 2   | `<child>/main.bicep`                                             | Add `enableTelemetry` param and `avmTelemetry` resource   |
| 3   | `<parent>/main.bicep`                                            | Add `enableReferencedModulesTelemetry` var, wire to child |
| 4   | `<child>/version.json`                                           | Create with version `0.1`                                 |
| 5   | `<child>/CHANGELOG.md`                                           | Create with initial `0.1.0` entry                         |
| 6   | `<parent>/CHANGELOG.md` (and up)                                 | Add patch version entry                                   |

---

## Quality Checks

Before completing, verify:

- [ ] Child module path is in `child-module-publish-allowed-list.json` (alphabetical order)
- [ ] `enableTelemetry` parameter exists in child `main.bicep` with default `true`
- [ ] `avmTelemetry` resource block uses correct telemetry ID prefix from CSV
- [ ] `#disable-next-line no-deployments-resources` comment precedes the telemetry resource
- [ ] Direct parent has `enableReferencedModulesTelemetry = false` variable
- [ ] Parent passes `enableTelemetry: enableReferencedModulesTelemetry` to child module deployment
- [ ] `version.json` exists in child folder with version `0.1`
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` exists in child folder with `0.1.0` initial entry
- [ ] All versioned parent changelogs updated with incremented patch version
- [ ] `Set-AVMModule` has been run on modules (parent and children) with a changed main.bicep file
- [ ] No README.md files were manually edited
